tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

OSINT accounts indicate that 5 vessels were attacked. Saudi and Qatar have confirmed attacks on their tankers. The US has already retaliated against Iran. Now we are watching Iran retaliate.

For those of you who have been reading my daily updates on the Strait of Hormuz, you knew from a week ago that escalation was coming in the Oman lane. We have now seen a material slowdown in inbound VLCCs, which all but guarantees a slowdown in loading for Saudi, UAE, Iraq, and Kuwait. Even Iran's own loading is slowing due to insufficient tankers returning.

To maintain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, Iran will have to enforce compliance with the Oman lane, which all but guarantees more conflict in the days/weeks ahead. The US can continue with airstrikes, but there are major limits. Just remember that if airstrikes can win wars, Trump wouldn't have needed to sign the MOU. 

In my view, further escalation is the most likely outcome; flows through the Oman lane are going to slow to a halt, and Brent short positioning is at all-time highs.

Powder keg in the making.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker table.

USS Boxer is now in the region, so ground assault is also an option now.

tweets.text:
This is massive.

We’ve talked about how crypto should be the most intuitive medium for agentic commerce, and now it’s happening across the web.

Money follows the path of least resistance.

Only a matter of time before agents prefer a network like $SUI with free stablecoin txs, which can make a huge difference when you’re talking about regularly settling hundreds and thousands of microtransactions across the web for fractions of a cent.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol. https://t.co/pvICtEIixj

tweets.text:
Everyone learns this eventually, and events become obsolete because you can just have the event at your place with the few people you actually give a fuck about

“The irony is the best part of every trip, by far, is saying “fuck this line let’s go back to the house and get in the pool.” and repeating it every night. I’m not sure why it feels like a defeat, going back to our forty million dollar airbnb.”

thread_tweets.text:
after thoughts, wow there is intense emotional debate, especially around the var, was at one point $65k deep in this trade, sold off a lot at 2.5 and 1.1 odds when it was egypt winning, but also kept a decent chunk for fun

my take on the var is its just obviously a foul, technically, but you have to leaning more neutral than a 100% foul because of how foul works in the first place, not every foul is called this wc, actually tons of defending teams tend to not get fouls calledin those areas of the pitch this world cup, if u watch ull understand immediately, but since it led to a goal it had to be checked, 

i think it just really made ppl hate var this game was that egypt went on to score what imo was 1 of the best counter attack goal of the world cup precise passing, speed, dribbling, perfect shot on goal, they made it too pretty to be disallowed so now all the fans hate it but it is what it is

also that lautaro martinez cross, a lot of people crossing the ball is just a rough area and pray, he only had 1 option in the box, and it couldnt been more perfect, tbh top 1 cross for sure this wc for me, i cant possibly see how this one will be topped
